title: Tensor: Type and Value
analysis: Task 228 adds a Tensor variant to both the Type and Value enums in crates/reify-types. The Type variant stores rank (number of nesting levels), n (dimension per level), and quantity (the element type, boxed). This follows the same pattern as the recently-added Point { n, quantity } and Vector { n, quantity } from Task 222. The Value variant stores elements as a Vec<Value> with recursive nesting: rank-1 tensors hold n scalar Values, rank-k tensors hold n Tensor values of rank-(k-1). Type derives Debug/Clone/PartialEq/Eq/Hash automatically — no manual trait impls needed. Value has custom PartialEq, Ord, Display, and content_hash implementations that each need a new Tensor arm. content_hash uses domain-separated tag bytes; tags 0-13 are taken, so Tensor gets tag [14]. Value Ord uses type_tags 0-12; Tensor gets tag 13. No changes to lib.rs re-exports are needed since Type and Value are already re-exported as whole enums — new variants are automatically visible. The Display format for Type::Tensor follows the Point/Vector convention of encoding parameters in the name: Tensor{rank}x{n}<{quantity}>. For Value::Tensor, display uses bracket notation identical to List: [elem, elem, ...], which naturally nests for higher-rank tensors.

steps.description: Write failing test for Type::tensor(rank, n, q) factory method: assert Type::tensor(2, 3, Type::length()) produces Type::Tensor { rank: 2, n: 3, quantity: Box::new(Type::length()) }, and Type::tensor(1, 4, Type::Real) produces the corresponding variant.

Add Tensor { rank: usize, n: usize, quantity: Box<Type> } variant to Type enum (after Vector at line ~41 in ty.rs). Add pub fn tensor(rank: usize, n: usize, quantity: Type) -> Self factory method in impl Type block. This also implicitly provides Eq/Hash via existing #[derive].
Write failing test for Type::Tensor Display: assert Tensor(2,3,length) displays as 'Tensor2x3<Scalar[m]>', Tensor(1,4,Real) as 'Tensor1x4<Real>', Tensor(3,2,Int) as 'Tensor3x2<Int>'.
Add Display match arm for Type::Tensor in fmt::Display impl: write!(f, "Tensor{}x{}<{}>", rank, n, quantity). Follows Point/Vector display pattern of embedding parameters in the type name.

Write failing test for Type::Tensor Eq and Hash: (a) Tensor(2,3,Length) == Tensor(2,3,Length), (b) Tensor(2,3,Length) != Tensor(1,3,Length) (different rank), (c) Tensor(2,3,Length) != Tensor(2,4,Length) (different n), (d) Tensor(2,3,Length) != Tensor(2,3,Real) (different quantity), (e) Tensor != Vector/Point with same n/quantity, (f) HashMap insert+lookup consistency.

No code change needed — Eq and Hash are provided by #[derive] on Type. Tests pass after step 2. Also add test asserting Type::Tensor is not numeric (is_numeric() returns false), which passes from existing matches! macro exclusion.

Write failing test for Value::Tensor construction and PartialEq: (a) rank-1 tensor with 3 length scalars equals itself rebuilt, (b) tensors with different elements are unequal, (c) rank-2 nested tensor (Tensor of Tensors) equals itself, (d) Tensor([Int(1), Int(2)]) != List([Int(1), Int(2)]) — distinct variants.
Add Tensor(Vec<Value>) variant to Value enum. Add PartialEq arm: (Value::Tensor(a), Value::Tensor(b)) => a == b. Recursive equality through Vec<Value>::PartialEq.

Write failing test for Value::Tensor Display: rank-1 tensor of 3 length scalars displays as '[0.08 m, 0.1 m, 0.12 m]', rank-2 nested tensor of Ints displays as '[[1, 2], [3, 4]]' with natural bracket nesting.
Add Display match arm for Value::Tensor: write bracket-delimited comma-separated format '[elem1, elem2, ...]', identical pattern to Value::List Display arm.

Write failing test for Value::Tensor content_hash: (a) determinism — identical rank-1 tensors produce identical hashes, (b) different elements produce different hashes, (c) Tensor hash differs from List hash with identical elements (tag [14] vs [7] separation), (d) nested rank-2 tensor hash is deterministic.

Add content_hash arm for Value::Tensor: tag [14], combine element count as u64 LE bytes, then combine each element's content_hash. Follows List pattern (tag [7]) but with distinct tag byte for domain separation.

Write failing test for Value::Tensor Ord: (a) Tensor type_tag (13) > Lambda type_tag (12) — cross-type ordering, (b) within-type lexicographic comparison of elements, (c) shorter tensor < longer tensor with same prefix elements.
Add Tensor to type_tag() function in Ord impl as tag 13. Add Ord match arm: (Value::Tensor(a), Value::Tensor(b)) => a.cmp(b) for lexicographic Vec comparison.
Write integration test entries in exports.rs verifying Type::Tensor direct construction, Type::tensor() factory, and Value::Tensor construction are accessible from crate root.

Add Tensor entries to all_m5_types_exported test in exports.rs: Type::Tensor { rank, n, quantity } direct construction, Type::tensor() factory call, and Value::Tensor(vec![...]) construction. Run full cargo test -p reify-types to verify everything passes end-to-end.

design_decisions.decision: Type variant: Tensor { rank: usize, n: usize, quantity: Box<Type> }
design_decisions.rationale: Matches task spec exactly. Follows Point/Vector pattern of { n: usize, quantity: Box<Type> } with added rank parameter. All dimensions of the tensor are the same size n (uniform tensor), consistent with mathematical rank-r tensors over an n-dimensional space.

design_decisions.decision: Value variant: Tensor(Vec<Value>) with recursive nesting
design_decisions.rationale: Task specifies 'nested Vec<Value> (rank levels of nesting, innermost elements are Scalars)'. Rank-1 tensors hold n scalar Values; rank-k tensors hold n Tensor values of rank-(k-1). The type system (Type::Tensor) carries shape info, so Value doesn't duplicate rank/n. This is the simplest representation matching the spec.

Mirrors Point/Vector display pattern (PointN<Q>, VectorN<Q>) but encodes both rank and n since tensors have two size parameters. The 'x' separator is natural for dimension notation in engineering contexts.

design_decisions.decision: Display format: [elem, elem, ...] for Value::Tensor (same as List)
design_decisions.rationale: Tensor values display as bracket-delimited arrays. Rank-2+ tensors display nested brackets automatically via recursive Display on inner Tensor values. Visually identical to List display, but distinguished at the type level. This is the natural mathematical notation.
Tags 0-9 are used by existing Value variants, [10] is exclusively reserved for Satisfaction, [11] is Option, [12] is Lambda, [13] is Field. Tag [14] is the next available sequential tag.
Current type_tags are 0-12 (Undef through Lambda). Tensor gets 13, placing it after Lambda in cross-type ordering. This is the next sequential tag, maintaining the established convention.

reuse.what: Point/Vector variant pattern in Type enum
reuse.how: Tensor follows the same { n: usize, quantity: Box<Type> } structural pattern with an additional rank:usize field. Factory method, Display arm, and test patterns are direct templates.

reuse.what: Existing #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)] on Type
reuse.how: Tensor variant gets Eq/Hash for free from the derive macro — no manual implementation needed for Type.
Value::Tensor Display uses identical bracket-delimited comma-separated format as Value::List.
Tensor content_hash follows the same tag + length + element-hash-combine pattern as List, but with tag [14] instead of [7].
Tensor PartialEq delegates to Vec<Value>::eq, and Ord delegates to Vec<Value>::cmp, identical to the List arms.
Tensor Eq/Hash tests follow the same structure: construct equal values and assert_eq, construct different values and assert_ne, verify HashMap insert+lookup.
Add Tensor entries following the Point/Vector export test pattern: direct construction + factory method + Value variant.
